types.name: Query
types.description:
Combined theme queries

Public queries (no auth):
- themes: Browse theme store
- themeDetails: View single theme
- publicPuckData: Fetch puck data for storefront (X-Store-Hostname header)

Authenticated queries (requires workspace):
- myThemes: User's theme library
- themeCustomization: Get customization for editor
- activeTheme: Currently published theme

fields.name: myThemes
fields.description: Get all themes in user's library (published + drafts)

fields.name: themeCustomization
fields.description: Get specific theme customization for Puck editor

fields.name: activeTheme
fields.description: Get currently published theme for workspace

fields.name: themeVersionHistory
fields.description: Get all timestamped snapshots representing previous versions or edits

fields.name: themes
fields.description: Browse theme store with pagination and filtering (PUBLIC)

fields.name: themeDetails
fields.description: Get detailed theme information by slug (PUBLIC)

fields.name: publicPuckData
fields.description: Fetch active theme's puck data for storefront (uses X-Store-Hostname header)

types.name: ThemeCustomizationType
types.description:
User's theme customization in their library

Contains user's puck data/config for the Puck editor
Workspace-scoped - only accessible to workspace owner

fields.name: template
fields.description: Master template being customized

fields.name: activeVersion
fields.description: The currently active snapshot version for this instance (Required)

fields.name: themeName
fields.description: User-friendly name for this theme (can be renamed)

fields.name: isActive
fields.description: Whether this theme is currently published and live on the workspace

fields.name: publishedAt
fields.description: When this customization was last published to production

fields.name: lastEditedAt
fields.description: When customization was last modified

fields.name: puckConfig
fields.description: Puck Editor configuration

fields.name: puckData
fields.description: Puck layout snapshot data

fields.name: isPasswordProtected
fields.description: Whether the published storefront is password protected

fields.name: storefrontPassword
fields.description: Current password for password-protected storefront (for merchant to share)

types.name: Node
types.description: An object with an ID

fields.name: id
fields.description: The ID of the object

types.name: ID
types.description: The `ID` scalar type represents a unique identifier, often used to refetch an object or as key for a cache. The ID type appears in a JSON response as a String; however, it is not intended to be human-readable. When expected as an input type, any string (such as `"4"`) or integer (such as `4`) input value will be accepted as an ID.

types.name: ThemeDetailsType
types.description:
Detailed theme type for single theme view

Contains full metadata for decision-making
Still NO puck data (user gets that after adding to library)

fields.name: currentVersion
fields.description: The latest stabilized global version of this theme

fields.name: name
fields.description: Unique name for the template

fields.name: slug
fields.description: URL-friendly version of the name

fields.name: description
fields.description: Detailed description of the template

fields.name: templateType
fields.description: Type of business this template is designed for

fields.name: priceTier
fields.description: Pricing category for the template

fields.name: priceAmount
fields.description: Price in FCFA for paid/exclusive templates

fields.name: status
fields.description: Current status of the template

fields.name: demoUrl
fields.description: Live demo URL for previewing the template

fields.name: previewImage
fields.description: Preview image URL from CDN

fields.name: viewCount
fields.description: Number of times this template has been viewed

fields.name: downloadCount
fields.description: Number of times this template has been downloaded

fields.name: activeUsageCount
fields.description: Number of active workspaces using this template

fields.name: workspaceTypes
fields.description: List of compatible workspace types (e.g., ['store', 'services'])

fields.name: tags
fields.description: Tags for categorization and search

fields.name: compatibility
fields.description: Technology compatibility requirements (nextjs, react, etc.)

fields.name: author
fields.description: Template author or creator

fields.name: license
fields.description: Template license type

types.name: String
types.description: The `String` scalar type represents textual data, represented as UTF-8 character sequences. The String type is most often used by GraphQL to represent free-form human-readable text.

types.name: ThemeTemplateTemplateTypeChoices
types.description: An enumeration.

enumValues.name: ECOMMERCE
enumValues.description: E-commerce

enumValues.name: SERVICES
enumValues.description: Services

enumValues.name: BLOG
enumValues.description: Blog

enumValues.name: RESTAURANT
enumValues.description: Restaurant

types.name: ThemeTemplatePriceTierChoices
types.description: An enumeration.

enumValues.name: FREE
enumValues.description: Free

enumValues.name: PAID
enumValues.description: Paid

enumValues.name: EXCLUSIVE
enumValues.description: Exclusive

types.name: Decimal
types.description: The `Decimal` scalar type represents a python Decimal.

types.name: ThemeTemplateStatusChoices
types.description: An enumeration.

enumValues.name: DRAFT
enumValues.description: Draft

enumValues.name: ACTIVE
enumValues.description: Active

enumValues.name: DEPRECATED
enumValues.description: Deprecated

types.name: Int
types.description: The `Int` scalar type represents non-fractional signed whole numeric values. Int can represent values between -(2^31) and 2^31 - 1.

types.name: DateTime
types.description:
The `DateTime` scalar type represents a DateTime
value as specified by
[iso8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601).

types.name: JSONString
types.description:
Allows use of a JSON String for input / output from the GraphQL schema.

Use of this type is *not recommended* as you lose the benefits of having a defined, static
schema (one of the key benefits of GraphQL).

types.name: FeatureCategoryType
types.description:
Feature category with items list
Used for nested features structure

types.name: ShowcaseSectionType
types.description:
Showcase section with title, description, and image
Used in theme details page

types.name: Boolean
types.description: The `Boolean` scalar type represents `true` or `false`.

types.name: ThemeVersionType
types.description:
Snapshot history for a specific Theme Customization instance.

Exposes past saves and system updates that can be rolled back to.

fields.name: customization
fields.description: The theme instance this snapshot belongs to

fields.name: versionNumber
fields.description: Version identifier (e.g., 1.0.0, snapshot-2023)

fields.name: status
fields.description: Current status of this version

fields.name: isSystemUpdate
fields.description: True if this snapshot was generated by an upstream theme update, False if saved by merchant

fields.name: migrationLog
fields.description: Logs or details about the data migration process if applicable

fields.name: puckDataSnapshot
fields.description: Full snapshot of puck layout

types.name: ThemeTemplateVersionStatusChoices
types.description: An enumeration.

enumValues.name: DRAFT
enumValues.description: Draft

enumValues.name: ACTIVE
enumValues.description: Active

enumValues.name: DEPRECATED
enumValues.description: Deprecated

types.name: ThemeTemplateVersionCreatedByActorChoices
types.description: An enumeration.

enumValues.name: SYSTEM
enumValues.description: System

enumValues.name: MERCHANT
enumValues.description: Merchant

fields.name: pageInfo
fields.description: Pagination data for this connection.

fields.name: edges
fields.description: Contains the nodes in this connection.

types.name: PageInfo
types.description: The Relay compliant `PageInfo` type, containing data necessary to paginate this connection.

fields.name: hasNextPage
fields.description: When paginating forwards, are there more items?

fields.name: hasPreviousPage
fields.description: When paginating backwards, are there more items?

fields.name: startCursor
fields.description: When paginating backwards, the cursor to continue.

fields.name: endCursor
fields.description: When paginating forwards, the cursor to continue.

types.name: ThemeTypeEdge
types.description: A Relay edge containing a `ThemeType` and its cursor.

fields.name: node
fields.description: The item at the end of the edge

fields.name: cursor
fields.description: A cursor for use in pagination

types.name: ThemeType
types.description:
Public theme type for theme store listing

Contains light metadata for browsing
NO puck data (that's only for user customizations)

fields.name: currentVersion
fields.description: The latest stabilized global version of this theme

fields.name: name
fields.description: Unique name for the template

fields.name: slug
fields.description: URL-friendly version of the name

fields.name: description
fields.description: Detailed description of the template

fields.name: templateType
fields.description: Type of business this template is designed for

fields.name: priceTier
fields.description: Pricing category for the template

fields.name: priceAmount
fields.description: Price in FCFA for paid/exclusive templates

fields.name: status
fields.description: Current status of the template

fields.name: demoUrl
fields.description: Live demo URL for previewing the template

fields.name: previewImage
fields.description: Preview image URL from CDN

fields.name: viewCount
fields.description: Number of times this template has been viewed

fields.name: downloadCount
fields.description: Number of times this template has been downloaded

fields.name: activeUsageCount
fields.description: Number of active workspaces using this template

types.name: PuckDataResponse
types.description: Response type for public puck data query

types.name: Mutation
types.description:
Theme mutations (all require authentication + workspace scoping)

- addTheme: Clone theme to library
- updateThemeCustomization: Save Puck edits
- publishTheme: Make theme live
- unpublishTheme: Take theme offline
- deleteTheme: Remove from library
- duplicateTheme: Copy for experimentation
- renameTheme: Change theme name

fields.name: addTheme
fields.description:
Add theme to user's library (clone from theme store)

Shopify pattern: "Use theme" button in theme store
Creates draft customization with cloned puck data

fields.name: updateThemeCustomization
fields.description:
Update theme customization (save Puck editor changes)

Auto-saves from Puck editor every 30s or manual save
Updates puck_data and/or puck_config

fields.name: publishTheme
fields.description:
Publish theme (make it live on workspace)

Shopify pattern: Only one published theme at a time
Auto-unpublishes any other active theme

fields.name: unpublishTheme
fields.description:
Unpublish theme (take it offline)

Workspace will have no active theme until another is published
Use case: Maintenance mode or switching themes

fields.name: deleteTheme
fields.description:
Delete theme from library

Shopify pattern: Permanent deletion (no archive)
Cannot delete active theme - must publish another first

fields.name: duplicateTheme
fields.description:
Duplicate theme for experimentation

Shopify pattern: Copy theme to try variations
Creates new draft with copied puck data

fields.name: renameTheme
fields.description:
Rename theme in library

User-friendly labels for theme organization

fields.name: rollbackThemeVersion
fields.description:
Rollback to a previous snapshot of the theme.

Sets the ThemeInstance pointer back to an older ThemeVersion.
Instant switch. No migration needed.

fields.name: applyThemeUpdate
fields.description:
Triggers an asynchronous worker to safely deep-merge the global Template update
into the user's active theme customization snapshot.

types.name: AddTheme
types.description:
Add theme to user's library (clone from theme store)

Shopify pattern: "Use theme" button in theme store
Creates draft customization with cloned puck data

types.name: UpdateThemeCustomization
types.description:
Update theme customization (save Puck editor changes)

Auto-saves from Puck editor every 30s or manual save
Updates puck_data and/or puck_config

types.name: UpdateThemeCustomizationInput
types.description:
Input for updating theme customization

Updates user's puck data and config from Puck editor
Both fields optional - update only what changed

types.name: PublishTheme
types.description:
Publish theme (make it live on workspace)

Shopify pattern: Only one published theme at a time
Auto-unpublishes any other active theme

types.name: UnpublishTheme
types.description:
Unpublish theme (take it offline)

Workspace will have no active theme until another is published
Use case: Maintenance mode or switching themes

types.name: DeleteTheme
types.description:
Delete theme from library

Shopify pattern: Permanent deletion (no archive)
Cannot delete active theme - must publish another first

types.name: DuplicateTheme
types.description:
Duplicate theme for experimentation

Shopify pattern: Copy theme to try variations
Creates new draft with copied puck data

types.name: RenameTheme
types.description:
Rename theme in library

User-friendly labels for theme organization

types.name: RollbackThemeVersion
types.description:
Rollback to a previous snapshot of the theme.

Sets the ThemeInstance pointer back to an older ThemeVersion.
Instant switch. No migration needed.

types.name: ApplyThemeUpdate
types.description:
Triggers an asynchronous worker to safely deep-merge the global Template update
into the user's active theme customization snapshot.

types.name: __Schema
types.description: A GraphQL Schema defines the capabilities of a GraphQL server. It exposes all available types and directives on the server, as well as the entry points for query, mutation, and subscription operations.

fields.name: types
fields.description: A list of all types supported by this server.

fields.name: queryType
fields.description: The type that query operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: mutationType
fields.description: If this server supports mutation, the type that mutation operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: subscriptionType
fields.description: If this server supports subscription, the type that subscription operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: directives
fields.description: A list of all directives supported by this server.

types.name: __Type
types.description:
The fundamental unit of any GraphQL Schema is the type. There are many kinds of types in GraphQL as represented by the `__TypeKind` enum.

Depending on the kind of a type, certain fields describe information about that type. Scalar types provide no information beyond a name, description and optional `specifiedByURL`, while Enum types provide their values. Object and Interface types provide the fields they describe. Abstract types, Union and Interface, provide the Object types possible at runtime. List and NonNull types compose other types.

types.name: __TypeKind
types.description: An enum describing what kind of type a given `__Type` is.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a scalar.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an object. `fields` and `interfaces` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an interface. `fields`, `interfaces`, and `possibleTypes` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a union. `possibleTypes` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an enum. `enumValues` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an input object. `inputFields` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: LIST
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a list. `ofType` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: NON_NULL
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a non-null. `ofType` is a valid field.

types.name: __Field
types.description: Object and Interface types are described by a list of Fields, each of which has a name, potentially a list of arguments, and a return type.

types.name: __InputValue
types.description: Arguments provided to Fields or Directives and the input fields of an InputObject are represented as Input Values which describe their type and optionally a default value.

fields.name: defaultValue
fields.description: A GraphQL-formatted string representing the default value for this input value.

types.name: __EnumValue
types.description: One possible value for a given Enum. Enum values are unique values, not a placeholder for a string or numeric value. However an Enum value is returned in a JSON response as a string.

types.name: __Directive
types.description:
A Directive provides a way to describe alternate runtime execution and type validation behavior in a GraphQL document.

In some cases, you need to provide options to alter GraphQL's execution behavior in ways field arguments will not suffice, such as conditionally including or skipping a field. Directives provide this by describing additional information to the executor.

types.name: __DirectiveLocation
types.description: A Directive can be adjacent to many parts of the GraphQL language, a __DirectiveLocation describes one such possible adjacencies.

enumValues.name: QUERY
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a query operation.

enumValues.name: MUTATION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a mutation operation.

enumValues.name: SUBSCRIPTION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a subscription operation.

enumValues.name: FIELD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment definition.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_SPREAD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment spread.

enumValues.name: INLINE_FRAGMENT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an inline fragment.

enumValues.name: VARIABLE_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a variable definition.

enumValues.name: SCHEMA
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a schema definition.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a scalar definition.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an object type definition.

enumValues.name: FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field definition.

enumValues.name: ARGUMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an argument definition.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an interface definition.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a union definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM_VALUE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum value definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object type definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object field definition.

directives.name: include
directives.description: Directs the executor to include this field or fragment only when the `if` argument is true.

directives.name: skip
directives.description: Directs the executor to skip this field or fragment when the `if` argument is true.

directives.name: deprecated
directives.description: Marks an element of a GraphQL schema as no longer supported.

args.name: reason
args.description: Explains why this element was deprecated, usually also including a suggestion for how to access supported similar data. Formatted using the Markdown syntax, as specified by [CommonMark](https://commonmark.org/).

directives.name: specifiedBy
directives.description: Exposes a URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.

args.name: url
args.description: The URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.

directives.name: oneOf
directives.description: Indicates an Input Object is a OneOf Input Object.
